implementation_notes: Implementation via task-work delegation. Files planned: 0, Files actual: 0
requirements_addressed: AC-001: main() entry point with __name__ guard
AC-002: Recursive scanning of 5 status directories
AC-004: Schema v1 JSON with fixed key order and (status, id) sorting

completion_promises.criterion_text: installer/core/commands/lib/task_status_json.py exists with a main() entry point and if __name__ == '__main__': guard
completion_promises.evidence: Source file exists at installer/core/commands/lib/task_status_json.py with main() function (line 219) and if __name__ == '__main__': guard (line 258). CLI tested: python3 task_status_json.py --base-path . produces valid JSON dashboard.

completion_promises.criterion_text: Scans tasks/backlog/, tasks/in_progress/, tasks/in_review/, tasks/blocked/, tasks/completed/ recursively (including feature subfolders and archive folders)
completion_promises.evidence: scan_task_files() iterates STATUS_DIRS = ['backlog', 'in_progress', 'in_review', 'blocked', 'completed'] and uses Path.rglob('*.md') for recursive scanning. Tests verify nested feature subfolders (feat-sub/) and archive folders (2026-01/) are found. Live test against real project: 2174 tasks found across all 5 status dirs.

completion_promises.criterion_text: Parses frontmatter via task_utils.parse_task_frontmatter (no duplicate YAML-parsing logic)
completion_promises.evidence: parse_task_record() imports and calls parse_task_frontmatter from installer.core.commands.lib.task_utils. No YAML parsing logic is duplicated in task_status_json.py.

completion_promises.criterion_text: Emits schema v1 JSON to stdout with fixed key order and tasks sorted by (status, id)
completion_promises.evidence: OUTPUT_KEYS, TASK_KEYS, SUMMARY_KEYS define fixed key orders. build_dashboard() sorts tasks by (status, id) tuple. json.dumps(..., sort_keys=False) preserves insertion order. Tests verify schema_version='1.0', fixed key orders, and sort order. Live test output confirms correct structure.
